description: Figure 4 presents the initial microstructure and particle statistics of a hot-rolled Fe-Mn-Al-Ti-B low-density steel. Panel (a) shows a pseudo-3D SEM view along RD, TD, and ND, revealing primary and eutectic TiB₂ particles distributed in the metallic matrix. Panel (b) is a high-magnification SEM image of the RD surface, displaying coarse, regularly shaped primary TiB₂ particles with hexagonal prism morphology. Panel (c) provides a high-magnification SEM image where dashed red lines delineate Mn-rich austenite and Al-rich δ-ferrite regions, corroborated by EDS elemental maps of Mn, Cr, and Al (inset). Panel (d) displays XRD patterns identifying three phases: δ-ferrite, austenite, and hexagonal TiB₂. Panels (e–g) present micro-CT results on TiB₂ particle distributions: (e) spatial maps in four size domains (0.8–5, 5–10, 10–20, and 20–42.7 μm), (f) number fractions showing small particles (0.8–5 μm) dominate by count, and (g) volume fractions showing coarse particles (10–42.7 μm) account for the majority of the total particle volume. Collectively, the data establish that the steel possesses a dual-phase ferrite-austenite matrix with a bimodal TiB₂ distribution where coarse primary particles govern the microstructural volume fraction despite fine eutectic debris prevailing in number.
